comments.content: interesting take! what made you think about this? i've been pondering similar ideas but from a different angle 🤔 👀
yo that's a good point. what about this scenario though?

Building on that idea, The official explanation is what we are told, though I have questions. I've learned to be cautious about explanations that are too neat. Reality is messy, and when a narrative is this polished, it usually means someone has been editing out the parts that don't fit.

As I've noted before, While it's a complex topic, there's also a less obvious explanation. The problem with the dominant narrative is that it requires you to ignore too many inconvenient facts. Once you start asking the uncomfortable questions, the story changes significantly. That's my read on it anyway — happy to be proven wrong if someone has better data.
